snp3.1:1.2:
[72] I will tell of the going forth,
how the One with Vision went forth,
how, while investigating,
he approved of the going forth. (1)
snp3.1:1.3:
“This home life is confinement,
a basis for dust;
the going forth is like
an open space”:
having seen this, he went forth. (2)
snp3.1:1.4:
Having gone forth, he avoided
evil deeds with his body.
Having abandoned verbal misconduct,
he purified his livelihood. (3)
snp3.1:1.5:
The Buddha went to Rājagaha,
[the city] Giribbaja of the Magadhans.
Adorned with the excellent marks,
he walked for alms [in the city]. (4)
snp3.1:1.6:
While standing in his palace,
Bimbisāra caught sight of him.
Having seen him endowed with the marks,
he uttered this statement: (5)
snp3.1:1.7:
“Sirs, look at him,
handsome, stately, pure;
endowed with good conduct,
he looks a mere yoke’s length ahead. (6)
snp3.1:1.8:
“With downcast eyes, mindful,
it seems he is not from a low family.
Let the king’s messengers run out.
[Find out] where the bhikkhu will go.” (7)
snp3.1:1.9:
The messengers sent by the king
followed closely behind him,
[thinking:] “Where will the bhikkhu go?
Where is his dwelling place?” (8)
snp3.1:1.10:
Walking on alms without skipping houses,
his sense doors guarded, well restrained,
clearly comprehending, ever mindful,
he quickly filled up his bowl. (9) [73]
snp3.1:1.11:
Having walked on alms round,
the muni departed from the city.
He ascended Mount Paṇḍava:
“His dwelling place must be here!” (10)
snp3.1:1.12:
Having seen that he had entered his dwelling,
the messengers then
approached;
but one messenger returned
and informed the king: (11)
snp3.1:1.13:
“Great king, this bhikkhu
lives on the eastern side of Paṇḍava.
He sits in a mountain cavern
like a tiger, a bull, or a lion.” (12)
snp3.1:1.14:
Having heard the messenger’s report,
the khattiya set out in a fine vehicle.
Hurriedly, he departed
in the direction of Paṇḍava Mountain. (13)
snp3.1:1.15:
Having gone along the ground for vehicles,
the khattiya descended from his vehicle;
having approached on foot,
he arrived and entered. (14)
snp3.1:1.16:
Having sat down, the king greeted him
and then made polite conversation.
When the courtesies were finished,
he then uttered this statement: (15)
snp3.1:1.17:
“You are young, a lad,
a youth in the prime of life,
endowed with beauty and stature,
like a well-born khattiya. (16)
snp3.1:1.18:
“I will give you wealth — enjoy it
while shining at the head of an army,
accompanied by a troop of bull elephants.
Being asked, tell me your birth.” (17)
snp3.1:1.19:
“There is, O king, a country straight ahead,
on the slope of the Himalayas,
abounding in wealth and energy,
[ruled] by one native to the Kosalans. (18) [74]
snp3.1:1.20:
“I am by clan an Ādicca,
by birth I am a Sakyan.
I have gone forth from that family, O king,
not longing for sensual pleasures. (19)
snp3.1:1.21:
“Having seen the danger in sensual pleasures,
having seen renunciation as security,
I will go for the purpose of striving:
it is here that my mind delights.” (20)
